div1.entryFree:
Ordinance, 1) established rule or custom, observance: by custom and the o. of times, H5 II, 4, 83. why all these things change from their o. their natures, Caes. I, 3, 66.
2) divine dispensation: by God's just o. R3 IV, 4, 183. V, 5, 31. the superfluous and lust-dieted man, that slaves your o. Lr. IV, 1, 71. let o. come as the gods foresay it, Cymb. IV, 2, 145.
3) order, rank: when one but of my o. stood up, Cor. III, 2, 12.
3) ordnance, cannon: John II, 218. H5 II, 4, 126.
